epics.title: EPIC ENGINE-12: Scalable Async Runtime
epics.summary: Turn ENGINE-03's bounded Promise/microtask support into the full scalable async runtime with native completions, owner-thread continuation dispatch, deadlines, shutdown drain, backpressure, provider/offload integration, and stress evidence.
epics.goal: Sloppy can aim at a full scalable async runtime without overclaiming the current ENGINE-03 microtask-only boundary.
nonGoals: No Node/npm compatibility unless explicitly scoped.
No package-manager behavior.
No public timers/fetch/fs/process/Buffer API unless a task explicitly scopes it.
No public alpha docs, benchmark claims, or scalability claims before evidence.
No PostgreSQL or SQL Server JS bridge work before SQLite and core async evidence are solid.
No generated/build artifacts.
implementationRequirements: Start this EPIC only when a real external async source is ready to cross the runtime boundary.
Keep V8 entry on the owning engine thread.
Keep queues, buffers, resources, deadlines, shutdown, and provider/offload work bounded and diagnostic-driven.
Report default, V8-gated, live-provider, stress, and benchmark evidence separately.
acceptanceCriteria: Native completions resume JavaScript only on the owning V8 thread.
Pending request scopes survive native async work and clean up exactly once on success, rejection, cancellation, timeout, overflow, and shutdown.
Cancellation, deadlines, overflow, and provider failures produce deterministic diagnostics and safe error responses.
Queue capacity/backpressure is bounded, documented, and tested.
Stress/perf evidence demonstrates the intended scalability shape without making external benchmark claims.
recommendedPrGrouping: Chunk I - Scalable async runtime: ENGINE-12.A through ENGINE-12.D as one large-coherent runtime PR only when native completions/provider offload/deadline/shutdown work is ready to cross the runtime boundary.
